item.aggregated_output: /Users/roy/Documents/quant

# Unified Non-Level-2 Refresh With Legacy CLI Deprecation

## Summary
Refactor `quant-build-production-dataset` into the single canonical refresh entrypoint for all non-Level-2 data, and deprecate the old direct refresh/build CLIs that currently let users bypass the unified path. Default behavior remains **daily-first**: resolve the latest completed SSE trading day, refresh daily/reference data, build and gate execution-ready artifacts, and build/gate ML-ready only when the latest monthly boundary is complete.

## Key Changes
- Make `quant-build-production-dataset` the only default public refresh command:
  - optional `--end-date`; omitted resolves to the latest completed SSE open day using Asia/Shanghai time, the SSE trading calendar, and an after-close cutoff
  - generated labels and all derived paths come from a single `RefreshContext`, with no hardcoded `20260424` orchestration paths or downstream builder defaults
  - `--dry-run` emits the full step plan, resolved dates, labels, explicit input/output paths, input hashes when files already exist, skip reasons, and checkpoint reuse decisions
  - `--strict-full` fails if ML-ready cannot be completed
  - `--mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` makes operator intent explicit while keeping one public entrypoint
  - `--skip-baostock` reuses existing audited BaoStock/patch artifacts only; it fails if required active-identity artifacts are absent and records freshness/status
  - `--skip-gdelt-doc-api` skips slower GDELT document diagnostics only, while recording skipped status and any reused macro/event inputs
- Orchestrate all non-Level-2 families in dependency order:
  - Tushare validation, endpoint freshness probes, and latest-date resolution
  - stock master/BaoStock/sentinels
  - daily A-share store, ETF universe/store/reference
  - benchmark constituents/returns
  - fundamentals, PIT fundamentals, official industry manifest discovery/build, PIT industry, non-L2 reference
  - regimes, macro/events with GDELT diagnostics, noise layer
  - execution-ready build/gate
  - ML-ready build/gate only when monthly signal plus next rebalance-day data exists
- Add a shared internal orchestration layer so the unified command does not shell out to deprecated public CLIs. Old CLI modules can call the shared step functions only after an explicit bypass flag.
- Add checkpoint/resume semantics before broad deprecation lands:
  - stable step IDs
  - started/completed timestamps
  - normalized inputs, input hashes, output paths, and output hashes
  - `rebuilt`, `reused_existing`, `skipped`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated` statuses
  - resume decisions based on matching context labels, paths, hashes, and step versions

## Refresh Context
- Introduce one immutable `RefreshContext` created by `quant-build-production-dataset` before any step runs:
  - `start_date`
  - `requested_end_date`
  - `daily_end_date`
  - `monthly_signal_end`
  - `latest_rebalance_data_date`
  - generated label set for daily, monthly, execution-ready, ML-ready, benchmark, ETF proxy, regime, macro/event, PIT fundamentals, PIT industry, and non-L2 reference artifacts
  - all derived input/output paths for every builder and gate
  - command mode, skip flags, bypass policy, and checkpoint location
- Every orchestrated step must receive explicit context paths and labels. No step may rely on its standalone CLI defaults for production orchestration.
- Manifest output must record the actual input paths, input hashes, output paths, output hashes, generated labels, source date coverage, source staleness, and whether each artifact was rebuilt or reused.
- Production manifests and tests must verify recorded paths and hashes use the generated label for the requested refresh, not stale defaults from `20260424` or any other prior run.

## Date Resolution and Freshness
- Date resolution uses Asia/Shanghai local date/time and the SSE official trading calendar.
- If `--end-date` is omitted:
  - before the configured market-data completion cutoff, use the latest completed SSE open day strictly before today
  - after the cutoff, use today only if today is an SSE open day and required endpoint freshness probes confirm complete data
  - on weekends and SSE holidays, snap backward to the prior completed SSE open day
- If `--end-date` is provided:
  - preserve it as `requested_end_date`
  - set `daily_end_date` to that date only when it is an open day with complete endpoint freshness
  - otherwise snap `daily_end_date` backward to the prior completed open day and record `requested_end_date_status=closed_or_incomplete`
  - fail only when `--strict-full` or an explicit future date requires data that cannot be available
- The resolver must include a fixture where `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`, because SSE lists Labor Day closure from May 1 through May 5, 2026 and reopening on May 6.
- Extend Tushare validation beyond stock-basic access. Freshness probes must cover daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limit data, ETF daily, fund adjustment, fund NAV, benchmark data, and configured non-Level-2 families before the context is allowed to run live steps.

## Legacy CLI Deprecation
- Deprecate direct data-refresh/build entrypoints by requiring `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly:
  - `quant-build-monthly-history`, `quant-build-universe-history`, `quant-build-etf-universe`
  - `quant-backfill-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-reference`
  - `quant-backfill-fundamentals`, `quant-backfill-non-l2-reference`
  - `quant-backfill-benchmark-constituents`, `quant-backfill-benchmark-returns`
  - `quant-build-etf-proxy-returns`, `quant-build-regime-tags`, `quant-build-macro-event-layer`
  - `quant-build-noise-layer`, `quant-build-ml-dataset`, `quant-build-fundamental-features`
  - `quant-build-pit-industry`, `quant-build-official-industry-manifest`
  - `quant-build-baostock-reference`, `quant-build-baostock-sentinels`
  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset`
- Keep validation/gate commands active and not deprecated:
  - `quant-validate-tushare`
  - `quant-validate-store`
  - `quant-gate-ml-dataset`
  - `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset`
- Direct deprecated CLIs should fail fast with:
  - replacement command: `uv run quant-build-production-dataset`
  - escape hatch: rerun with `--allow-manual-data-step`
  - clear note that manual runs are for troubleshooting, diagnostics, or isolated reruns only
- `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` is a builder, so it is internal/orchestrator-only by default and requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when invoked directly. `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset` remains unrestricted.
- Manual bypass runs must preserve current standalone behavior only when real arguments are provided explicitly. Bypass mode must not silently use stale production defaults.
- `quant-build-official-industry-manifest` is part of the unified orchestration because PIT industry consumes its source manifest. If an operator bypass reuses an existing manifest, output JSON must record manifest path, source period coverage, source staleness, and reuse status.
- Update `AGENTS.md`, `docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md`, and current preprocessing docs so the unified command is the only documented default route.

## Public Interface
```bash
uv run quant-build-production-dataset
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --end-date 20260430
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --dry-run
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --strict-full
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode daily-refresh
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode monthly-refresh
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode full-refresh
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-baostock
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-gdelt-doc-api
```

Output JSON includes `requested_end_date`, `daily_end_date`, `requested_end_date_status`, `monthly_signal_end`, `monthly_boundary_status`, command mode, generated labels/paths, per-step statuses, checkpoint decisions, skipped/reused/stale/failed/gated states, execution-ready gate status, and ML-ready status.

For `--skip-baostock`, output JSON must still include the active stock-universe identity source, patched master path, audited patch source path, sentinel status, freshness, and whether the required artifacts were reused.

## Test Plan
- Dry-run tests:
  - generated plan contains no stale `20260424` paths for a new end date
  - all non-Level-2 data families appear by default
  - generated plan includes official industry manifest build/discovery before PIT industry
  - generated plan includes explicit generated inputs for production constants, ML dataset, execution-ready, ETF proxy, macro regimes, benchmark, labels, and gates
  - ML-ready is skipped with a clear reason when monthly boundary is incomplete
  - dry-run fixture covers a month-end-complete date
  - dry-run fixture covers an incomplete monthly boundary
- Deprecation tests:
  - each deprecated direct CLI exits nonzero without `--allow-manual-data-step`
  - deprecated direct CLIs run their existing behavior with the bypass flag
  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly
  - bypass runs require explicit real arguments for production-sensitive paths and do not fall back to stale defaults
  - gate/validation commands remain callable without bypass
- Resolver and behavior tests:
  - holiday/weekend resolves to prior completed open day
  - `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`
  - explicit closed `--end-date` preserves `requested_end_date`, snaps `daily_end_date` backward, and records the closed/incomplete status
  - endpoint freshness probes block live execution when daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limits, ETF daily, fund_adj, fund_nav, benchmark, or non-Level-2 families are stale
  - `--strict-full` fails when ML-ready would be skipped
  - daily-first mode can succeed with execution-ready gated and ML skipped
- Integration tests:
  - production orchestration uses an internal API with stubbed steps instead of `uv run` subprocess command lists
  - no deprecated console script names appear in the production execution path
  - every step receives explicit paths from `RefreshContext`
  - output schema distinguishes `skipped`, `reused_existing`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated`
  - partial daily-first success records execution-ready success and ML-ready skip without marking the full run failed
  - manifest tests assert recorded input paths and hashes match the generated refresh label
  - checkpoint/resume tests reuse only when step IDs, labels, paths, hashes, and step versions match
- Quality gates:
  ```bash
  uv run ruff check src tests
  uv run ruff format --check src tests
  uv run pyright
  uv run bandit -c pyproject.toml -r src/quantresearch
  uv run --group test pytest -q
  ```

## Assumptions
- “Deprecate” means direct manual CLIs remain available only behind `--allow-manual-data-step`, not removed from `pyproject.toml`.
- `quant-build-production-dataset` remains the public command name.
- Level-2 remains excluded.
- Canonical research-run pointers are not updated automatically.
- Unresolved questions: none.

    36	def run_preflight(
    37	    settings: Settings,
    38	    paths: PreflightPaths,

    40	    require_token: bool = False,
    41	    min_free_gb: float = 5.0,
    42	    manual_benchmark_path: Path | None = None,
    43	) -> dict[str, object]:
    44	    issues: list[ValidationIssue] = []

    48	        "daily_regimes": paths.daily_regimes,
    49	        "monthly_regimes": paths.monthly_regimes,

    52	        if not path.exists():

    55	            )

    57	        issues.append(ValidationIssue(IssueSeverity.PROMOTE_BLOCKER, "missing_tushare_token", "TUSHARE_TOKEN is not available"))
    58	    if manual_benchmark_path is not None and not manual_benchmark_path.exists():
    59	        issues.append(

    62	                "missing_manual_benchmark_file",

    66	        )

    72	            )
    73	        )
    74	    free_gb = shutil.disk_usage(paths.output_root).free / 1024**3
    75	    if free_gb < min_free_gb:
    76	        issues.append(
    77	            ValidationIssue(
    78	                IssueSeverity.PROMOTE_BLOCKER,
    79	                "low_disk_headroom",
    80	                "disk free space is below requested headroom",
    81	                {"free_gb": round(free_gb, 3), "min_free_gb": min_free_gb},
    82	            )
    83	        )
    84	    summary = summarize_issues(issues)
    85	    summary.update(
    86	        {
    87	            "input_paths": {name: str(path) for name, path in required_files.items()},
    88	            "output_root": str(paths.output_root),
    89	            "free_gb": round(free_gb, 3),
    90	            "can_live_backfill_benchmark": bool(settings.tushare_token),
    91	            "can_manual_import_benchmark": bool(manual_benchmark_path and manual_benchmark_path.exists()),
    92	        }
    93	    )
    94	    return summary

docs/51_institutional_strategy_map_and_enhanced_index_path.md:40:Enhanced indexing is the closest institutional analogue to the user's target. It is neither pure passive nor high-conviction active management. The manager starts with a benchmark, then allows limited deviations from that benchmark to seek modest excess return.
docs/51_institutional_strategy_map_and_enhanced_index_path.md:42:Goldman Sachs Asset Management describes alpha-enhanced equity as combining the cost effectiveness and predictability of passive equity with active risk management and alpha potential. Its 2025 note says these strategies closely track a benchmark, take active bets within pre-set tracking-error limits, and often operate around a 50-200 basis-point tracking-error budget. It also emphasizes that active positions are distributed across market caps, sectors, and geographies to avoid concentration and unintended exposures. Source: [Goldman Sachs Asset Management, alpha-enhanced core equity](https://am.gs.com/en-us/institutions/insights/article/2025/boosting-efficiency-core-equity-allocations-alpha-enhanced-approach).
docs/51_institutional_strategy_map_and_enhanced_index_path.md:118:CFA Institute's hedge fund strategy reading classifies hedge funds across equity-related, event-driven, relative value, opportunistic, specialist, and multi-manager strategies, and warns that hedge funds can add leverage, transparency, liquidity, fee, and strategy-specific risks. Source: [CFA Institute, Hedge Fund Strategies](https://www.cfainstitute.org/insights/professional-learning/refresher-readings/2026/hedge-fund-strategies).
docs/51_institutional_strategy_map_and_enhanced_index_path.md:226:CFA Institute warns that arbitrage-oriented hedge fund strategies often use significant leverage and may face stress-period risks. Source: [CFA Institute, Hedge Fund Strategies](https://www.cfainstitute.org/insights/professional-learning/refresher-readings/2026/hedge-fund-strategies).

    26	def _month_ranges(start_date: str, end_date: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    27	    start = pd.to_datetime(start_date, format="%Y%m%d")
    28	    end = pd.to_datetime(end_date, format="%Y%m%d")
    29	    ranges: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    30	    cursor = start
    31	    while cursor <= end:
    32	        month_end = (cursor + pd.offsets.MonthEnd(0)).normalize()
    33	        chunk_end = min(month_end, end)
    34	        ranges.append((cursor.strftime("%Y%m%d"), chunk_end.strftime("%Y%m%d")))
    35	        cursor = chunk_end + pd.Timedelta(days=1)
    36	    return ranges

   106	def leakage_prone_feature_columns(frame: pd.DataFrame) -> list[str]:
   107	    blocked: list[str] = []
   108	    for col in frame.columns:
   109	        col_text = str(col)
   110	        if col_text in LEAKAGE_PRONE_FEATURE_COLUMNS:
   111	            blocked.append(col_text)
   112	            continue
   113	        if col_text.startswith(LEAKAGE_PRONE_FEATURE_COLUMN_PREFIXES):
   114	            blocked.append(col_text)
   115	            continue
   116	        if col_text.startswith("benchmark_") and "_return" in col_text:
   117	            blocked.append(col_text)
   118	    return blocked

   166	def apply_stock_code_alias_policy(
   167	    monthly: pd.DataFrame,
   168	    stock_code_aliases: pd.DataFrame | None,
   169	    *,
   170	    mode: str = "drop_alias_rows",
   171	) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
   172	    if stock_code_aliases is None or stock_code_aliases.empty:
   173	        return monthly.copy(), pd.DataFrame(
   174	            columns=["alias_ts_code", "canonical_ts_code", "alias_status", "rows_affected", "policy_action", "evidence"]
   175	        )
   176	    aliases = stock_code_aliases.copy()
   177	    if "alias_status" not in aliases.columns:
   178	        aliases["alias_status"] = ""
   179	    aliases = aliases[aliases["alias_status"].astype(str).eq("confirmed_code_migration_alias")].copy()
   180	    if aliases.empty:
   181	        return monthly.copy(), pd.DataFrame(
   182	            columns=["alias_ts_code", "canonical_ts_code", "alias_status", "rows_affected", "policy_action", "evidence"]
   183	        )
   184	    alias_codes = set(aliases["alias_ts_code"].astype(str))
   185	    out = monthly.copy()
   186	    out["ts_code"] = out["ts_code"].astype(str)
   187	    rows = []
   188	    for row in aliases.itertuples(index=False):
   189	        alias_code = str(getattr(row, "alias_ts_code"))
   190	        canonical_code = str(getattr(row, "canonical_ts_code", ""))
   191	        rows_affected = int(out["ts_code"].eq(alias_code).sum())
   192	        rows.append(
   193	            {
   194	                "alias_ts_code": alias_code,
   195	                "canonical_ts_code": canonical_code,
   196	                "alias_status": str(getattr(row, "alias_status", "")),
   197	                "rows_affected": rows_affected,
   198	                "policy_action": mode,
   199	                "evidence": str(getattr(row, "evidence", "")),
   200	            }
   201	        )
   202	    if mode == "drop_alias_rows":
   203	        out = out[~out["ts_code"].isin(alias_codes)].copy()
   204	    else:
   205	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported stock code alias policy mode: {mode}")
   206	    return out.reset_index(drop=True), pd.DataFrame(rows)

    25	def build_monthly_schedule(pro, start_date: str, end_date: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    26	    end_plus = (pd.Timestamp(end_date) + pd.offsets.MonthEnd(1) + pd.Timedelta(days=10)).strftime("%Y%m%d")
    27	    cal = pro.trade_cal(
    28	        exchange="SSE",
    29	        start_date=start_date,
    30	        end_date=end_plus,
    31	        fields="exchange,cal_date,is_open,pretrade_date",
    32	    )
    33	    cal = cal[cal["is_open"] == 1].copy()
    34	    cal["cal_date"] = pd.to_datetime(cal["cal_date"], format="%Y%m%d")
    35	    cal = cal.sort_values("cal_date").reset_index(drop=True)
    36	    cal["month"] = cal["cal_date"].dt.to_period("M")
    37	
    38	    month_end = cal.groupby("month", as_index=False)["cal_date"].max()
    39	    month_end = month_end[month_end["cal_date"] <= pd.Timestamp(end_date)].copy()
    40	    month_end["signal_date"] = month_end["cal_date"].dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
    41	
    42	    all_days = cal["cal_date"].tolist()
    43	    next_dates: list[pd.Timestamp | pd.NaT] = []
    44	    for dt in month_end["cal_date"]:
    45	        next_dt = next((day for day in all_days if day > dt), pd.NaT)
    46	        next_dates.append(next_dt)
    47	    month_end["rebalance_date"] = pd.to_datetime(next_dates)
    48	    month_end = month_end.dropna(subset=["rebalance_date"]).copy()
    49	    month_end["rebalance_date"] = month_end["rebalance_date"].dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
    50	    month_end["month"] = month_end["month"].astype(str)
    51	    return month_end[["month", "signal_date", "rebalance_date"]].reset_index(drop=True)
    52	
    53	
    54	def build_monthly_history(
    55	    pro,
    56	    start_date: str,
    57	    end_date: str,
    58	    symbol_scope: str = "mainboard",
    59	    stock_master: pd.DataFrame | None = None,
    60	) -> MonthlyHistoryArtifacts:
    61	    if stock_master is not None:
    62	        master = stock_master.copy()
    63	    elif symbol_scope == "mainboard":
    64	        master = fetch_mainboard_master(pro)
    65	    elif symbol_scope == "all_a_share":
    66	        master = fetch_csi_a_share_master(pro)
    67	    else:
    68	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported symbol_scope: {symbol_scope}")
    69	    schedule = build_monthly_schedule(pro, start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)
    70	    namechange_history = fetch_namechange_history(pro, start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)
    71	    frames: list[pd.DataFrame] = []
    72	    for row in schedule.itertuples(index=False):
    73	        snapshot = build_mainboard_snapshot(
    74	            pro=pro,
    75	            master=master,
    76	            signal_date=row.signal_date,
    77	            rebalance_date=row.rebalance_date,
    78	            namechange_history=namechange_history,
    79	        )
    80	        snapshot["month"] = row.month
    81	        frames.append(snapshot)
    82	    if frames:
    83	        columns = list(dict.fromkeys(col for frame in frames for col in frame.columns))
    84	        concat_frames = [frame.dropna(axis=1, how="all") for frame in frames]
    85	        history = pd.concat(concat_frames, ignore_index=True).reindex(columns=columns)
    86	    else:
    87	        history = pd.DataFrame()
    88	    return MonthlyHistoryArtifacts(schedule=schedule, history=history)

    96	    write_frame_csv(artifacts.schedule, schedule_csv)
    97	    write_frame_csv(artifacts.history, history_csv)

    33	def enrich_monthly_history(history: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    34	    df = history.copy()
    35	    df["signal_date_dt"] = pd.to_datetime(df["signal_date"], format="%Y%m%d")
    36	    df = df.sort_values(["ts_code", "signal_date_dt"]).reset_index(drop=True)
    37	
    38	    grouped = df.groupby("ts_code", sort=False)
    39	    df["prev_signal_close"] = grouped["signal_close"].shift(1)
    40	    df["next_entry_open"] = grouped["entry_open"].shift(-1)
    41	    df["next_entry_down_limit"] = grouped["entry_down_limit"].shift(-1)
    42	    df["forward_return"] = df["next_entry_open"] / df["entry_open"] - 1.0
    43	    df["one_month_signal_return"] = df["signal_close"] / df["prev_signal_close"] - 1.0
    44	    df["exit_open_le_down_limit"] = (
    45	        df["next_entry_open"].notna() & df["next_entry_down_limit"].notna() & (df["next_entry_open"] <= df["next_entry_down_limit"] + 1e-9)
    46	    )
    47	    return df

    50	def _cross_section_filter(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    51	    if df.empty:
    52	        return df
    53	    amount_floor = df["signal_amount"].dropna().quantile(0.2)
    54	    mv_floor = df["circ_mv"].dropna().quantile(0.2)
    55	    return df[(df["signal_amount"] >= amount_floor) & (df["circ_mv"] >= mv_floor) & df["turnover_rate"].notna()].copy()

    63	def _return_stats(rets: pd.Series, prefix: str) -> dict[str, float]:
    64	    if rets.empty:
    65	        return {
    66	            f"{prefix}_mean_monthly_return": float("nan"),
    67	            f"{prefix}_annualized_return_arithmetic": float("nan"),
    68	            f"{prefix}_annualized_return_geometric": float("nan"),
    69	            f"{prefix}_annualized_volatility": float("nan"),
    70	            f"{prefix}_sharpe": float("nan"),
    71	            f"{prefix}_hit_rate": float("nan"),
    72	            f"{prefix}_max_drawdown": float("nan"),
    73	        }
    74	    wealth = (1.0 + rets).cumprod()
    75	    peak = wealth.cummax()
    76	    drawdown = wealth / peak - 1.0
    77	    ann_vol = float(rets.std(ddof=1) * np.sqrt(12.0)) if len(rets) > 1 else float("nan")
    78	    sharpe = float((rets.mean() / rets.std(ddof=1)) * np.sqrt(12.0)) if len(rets) > 1 and rets.std(ddof=1) > 0 else float("nan")
    79	    geo = -1.0 if wealth.iloc[-1] <= 0 else float(wealth.iloc[-1] ** (12.0 / len(rets)) - 1.0)
    80	    return {
    81	        f"{prefix}_mean_monthly_return": float(rets.mean()),
    82	        f"{prefix}_annualized_return_arithmetic": float(rets.mean() * 12.0),
    83	        f"{prefix}_annualized_return_geometric": geo,
    84	        f"{prefix}_annualized_volatility": ann_vol,
    85	        f"{prefix}_sharpe": sharpe,
    86	        f"{prefix}_hit_rate": float((rets > 0).mean()),
    87	        f"{prefix}_max_drawdown": float(drawdown.min()),
    88	    }

    91	def _compute_trade_costs(
    92	    prev_weights: dict[str, float],
    93	    target_weights: dict[str, float],
    94	    portfolio_value: float,
    95	    cost_model: CostModel,
    96	) -> dict[str, float | int]:
    97	    buy_turnover = 0.0
    98	    sell_turnover = 0.0
    99	    trade_cost_cny = 0.0
   100	    buy_names = 0
   101	    sell_names = 0
   102	    for ts_code in set(prev_weights) | set(target_weights):
   103	        delta = target_weights.get(ts_code, 0.0) - prev_weights.get(ts_code, 0.0)
   104	        if delta > 1e-12:
   105	            notional = portfolio_value * delta
   106	            broker_cost = max(
   107	                notional * cost_model.buy_broker_commission_rate,
   108	                cost_model.min_commission_cny,
   109	            )
   110	            market_cost = notional * cost_model.buy_market_cost_rate
   111	            trade_cost_cny += broker_cost + market_cost
   112	            buy_turnover += delta
   113	            buy_names += 1
   114	        elif delta < -1e-12:
   115	            notional = portfolio_value * (-delta)
   116	            broker_cost = max(
   117	                notional * cost_model.sell_broker_commission_rate,
   118	                cost_model.min_commission_cny,
   119	            )
   120	            market_cost = notional * cost_model.sell_market_cost_rate
   121	            trade_cost_cny += broker_cost + market_cost
   122	            sell_turnover += -delta
   123	            sell_names += 1
   124	    return {
   125	        "buy_turnover": float(buy_turnover),
   126	        "sell_turnover": float(sell_turnover),
   127	        "trade_cost_cny": float(trade_cost_cny),
   128	        "buy_names": int(buy_names),
   129	        "sell_names": int(sell_names),
   130	    }

   403	def build_rebalance_calendar(trade_dates: pd.Series | list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
   404	    dates = pd.Series(trade_dates, dtype="string").dropna().astype(str).str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True).drop_duplicates().sort_values()
   405	    frame = pd.DataFrame({"trade_date": dates.to_list()})
   406	    frame["trade_date_dt"] = pd.to_datetime(frame["trade_date"], format="%Y%m%d", errors="coerce")
   407	    frame = frame.dropna(subset=["trade_date_dt"]).sort_values("trade_date_dt").reset_index(drop=True)
   408	    frame["next_trade_date"] = frame["trade_date"].shift(-1).fillna("")
   409	    frame["calendar_year_week"] = frame["trade_date_dt"].dt.strftime("%G%V")
   410	    frame["calendar_month"] = frame["trade_date_dt"].dt.strftime("%Y%m")
   411	    frame["is_daily_rebalance"] = True
   412	    frame["is_weekly_rebalance"] = ~frame["calendar_year_week"].duplicated()
   413	    frame["is_monthly_rebalance"] = ~frame["calendar_month"].duplicated()
   414	    long_frames = []
   415	    for cadence, mask in (
   416	        ("daily", frame["is_daily_rebalance"]),
   417	        ("weekly", frame["is_weekly_rebalance"]),
   418	        ("monthly", frame["is_monthly_rebalance"]),
   419	    ):
   420	        part = frame.loc[mask, ["trade_date", "next_trade_date"]].copy()
   421	        part["cadence"] = cadence
   422	        long_frames.append(part)
   423	    out = pd.concat(long_frames, ignore_index=True)
   424	    out = out.rename(columns={"trade_date": "signal_date", "next_trade_date": "rebalance_date"})
   425	    ordered = out[["cadence", "signal_date", "rebalance_date"]].reset_index(drop=True)
   426	    return cast(pd.DataFrame, ordered)

    23	def _cost_scenario(name: str) -> CostScenario:
    24	    scenarios = {scenario.name: scenario for scenario in default_cost_scenarios()}
    25	    if name not in scenarios:
    26	        raise ValueError(f"unknown cost scenario: {name}")
    27	    return scenarios[name]

    30	def _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(series: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    31	    text = series.fillna("").astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)
    32	    parsed = pd.to_datetime(text, format="%Y%m%d", errors="coerce")
    33	    return parsed.dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
    34	
    35	
    36	def _trade_rate_cost(entry_date: object, exit_date: object, scenario: CostScenario, slippage_bps: float) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    37	    slippage = slippage_bps / 10000.0
    38	    buy_rate = scenario.buy_broker_rate + scenario.buy_other_rate + slippage
    39	    sell_rate = scenario.sell_broker_rate + effective_sell_other_rate(scenario, exit_date or entry_date) + slippage
    40	    return buy_rate, sell_rate, buy_rate + sell_rate

    43	def build_executable_labels(
    44	    monthly: pd.DataFrame,
    45	    *,
    46	    config: ExecutableLabelConfig = ExecutableLabelConfig(),
    47	    benchmark_returns: pd.DataFrame | None = None,
    48	) -> pd.DataFrame:
    49	    required = {"ts_code", "signal_date", config.entry_price_col}
    50	    missing = required - set(monthly.columns)
    51	    if missing:
    52	        raise ValueError(f"label frame missing columns: {sorted(missing)}")
    53	    out = monthly[["ts_code", "signal_date"]].copy()
    54	    out["label_horizon"] = config.horizon_name
    55	    out["label_start_date"] = (
    56	        _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(monthly[config.label_start_col])
    57	        if config.label_start_col in monthly.columns
    58	        else _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(monthly["signal_date"])
    59	    )
    60	    if config.label_end_col in monthly.columns:
    61	        out["label_end_date"] = _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(monthly[config.label_end_col])
    62	    elif "rebalance_date" in monthly.columns:
    63	        out["label_end_date"] = _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(monthly.groupby("ts_code", sort=False)["rebalance_date"].shift(-1))
    64	    else:
    65	        out["label_end_date"] = _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(monthly.groupby("ts_code", sort=False)["signal_date"].shift(-1))
    66	
    67	    entry = pd.to_numeric(monthly[config.entry_price_col], errors="coerce")
    68	    if config.exit_price_col in monthly.columns:
    69	        exit_ = pd.to_numeric(monthly[config.exit_price_col], errors="coerce")
    70	    elif "forward_return" in monthly.columns:
    71	        exit_ = entry * (1.0 + pd.to_numeric(monthly["forward_return"], errors="coerce"))
    72	    else:
    73	        exit_ = monthly.groupby("ts_code", sort=False)[config.entry_price_col].shift(-1)
    74	    raw_return = exit_ / entry - 1.0
    75	    if "forward_return" in monthly.columns:
    76	        raw_return = pd.to_numeric(monthly["forward_return"], errors="coerce").fillna(raw_return)
    77	    out["entry_price"] = entry
    78	    out["exit_price"] = exit_
    79	    out["holding_period_return"] = raw_return.replace([np.inf, -np.inf], np.nan)
    80	    scenario = _cost_scenario(config.cost_scenario_name)
    81	    rates = [
    82	        _trade_rate_cost(start, end, scenario, config.one_way_slippage_bps)
    83	        for start, end in zip(out["label_start_date"], out["label_end_date"], strict=False)
    84	    ]
    85	    out["buy_cost_rate"] = [item[0] for item in rates]
    86	    out["sell_cost_rate"] = [item[1] for item in rates]
    87	    out["round_trip_cost_rate"] = [item[2] for item in rates]
    88	    out["executable_return"] = (1.0 + out["holding_period_return"]) * (1.0 - out["sell_cost_rate"]) / (1.0 + out["buy_cost_rate"]) - 1.0
    89	    out["entry_blocked"] = monthly.get("entry_open_ge_up_limit", pd.Series(False, index=monthly.index)).fillna(False).astype(bool)
    90	    out["exit_blocked"] = monthly.get("exit_open_le_down_limit", pd.Series(False, index=monthly.index)).fillna(False).astype(bool)
    91	    out["signal_suspended"] = monthly.get("is_suspended_signal", pd.Series(False, index=monthly.index)).fillna(False).astype(bool)
    92	    out["entry_suspended"] = monthly.get("is_suspended_entry", pd.Series(False, index=monthly.index)).fillna(False).astype(bool)
    93	    quality_excluded = monthly.get("exclude_from_label", pd.Series(False, index=monthly.index))
    94	    out["quality_excluded"] = pd.Series(quality_excluded, index=monthly.index).astype("boolean").fillna(False).to_numpy(dtype=bool)
    95	    out["label_reason_codes"] = ""
    96	    blocked_reasons = {
    97	        "entry_blocked": "entry_limit_up_blocked",
    98	        "exit_blocked": "exit_limit_down_blocked",
    99	        "signal_suspended": "suspended_signal",
   100	        "entry_suspended": "suspended_entry",
   101	        "quality_excluded": "quality_excluded",
   102	    }
   103	    for col, reason in blocked_reasons.items():
   104	        mask = out[col].fillna(False).astype(bool)
   105	        out.loc[mask & out["label_reason_codes"].eq(""), "label_reason_codes"] = reason
   106	        out.loc[mask & out["label_reason_codes"].ne(reason) & out["label_reason_codes"].ne(""), "label_reason_codes"] += f"|{reason}"
   107	    out["exclude_from_label"] = (
   108	        out["entry_blocked"] | out["entry_suspended"] | out["quality_excluded"] | out["entry_price"].isna() | out["exit_price"].isna()
   109	    )
   110	    if benchmark_returns is not None and not benchmark_returns.empty:
   111	        bench = benchmark_returns.copy()
   112	        bench["signal_date"] = bench["signal_date"].astype(str)
   113	        out = out.merge(bench, on="signal_date", how="left")
   114	        for col in [c for c in bench.columns if c.endswith("_return") and c != "signal_date"]:
   115	            out[f"active_{col}"] = out["executable_return"] - out[col]
   116	    return out

1. Critical: The plan assumes a new internal orchestration layer with `RefreshContext`, dynamic date resolution, checkpoint/resume metadata, and rich JSON output, but the current production entrypoint is still a thin subprocess wrapper with fixed `20260424` defaults and `uv run` shell-outs for every step. It currently prints only `status`, `output_label`, `ml_ready_dir`, and `commands`, so none of the requested `requested_end_date` / `daily_end_date` / `monthly_signal_end` / step-status schema exists yet. See [data-plan.md:4](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md#L4), [data-plan.md:8](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md#L8), [data-plan.md:24](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md#L24), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:41](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py#L41), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:71](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py#L71), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:208](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py#L208).

2. High: The plan’s Tushare freshness contract is not what `quant-validate-tushare` does today. The current implementation only calls `stock_basic` and reports A-share counts plus a mainboard diagnostic; it does not probe daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limit, ETF, fund_adj, fund_nav, or benchmark freshness, and it does not gate live execution. See [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py:12](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py#L12), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py:17](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py#L17), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py:24](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py#L24), [docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md:40](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md#L40), and the plan’s freshness scope at [data-plan.md:16](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md#L16) and [data-plan.md:58](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md#L58).

3. High: The date-resolution behavior in the plan is not backed by the current code path. Preflight only checks static local files and disk space, while the production CLI hardcodes `20260424`-anchored defaults for `--end-date`, monthly-history paths, regime tags, ML-ready output, and downstream step inputs. I did not find a current Asia/Shanghai calendar resolver, cutoff logic, or the `2026-05-02 -> 20260430` fixture in the reviewed implementation surface. See [src/quantresearch/preprocessing/preflight.py:11](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/preflight.py#L11), [src/quantresearch/preprocessing/preflight.py:26](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/preflight.py#L26), [src/quantresearch/preprocessing/preflight.py:36](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/preflight.py#L36), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:42](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py#L42), [src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:47](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py#L47), [docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md:141](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md#L141).

4. High: The orchestrated family scope in the plan is much broader than the current production command.    138	- Paid PIT Shenwan/CITIC industry history: defer unless `CAPCO_CSRC_L1` neutralization improves out-of-sample evidence enough to justify a finer, paid industry taxonomy. The current default remains official CAPCO/CSRC L1 with BaoStock as a reference/check layer.
   139	- Paid macro consensus/surprise history: defer unless macro-surprise features become a real research path. Actual macro releases and official event anchors are allowed as diagnostics with strict `known_at_ts`; `surprise` stays null without licensed consensus/forecast history.
   140	- GDELT 1.0 or legacy raw-file backfill: defer unless old media-intensity diagnostics become necessary. The current event truth for 2008-2016 remains exact official anchors, with GDELT DOC API extracts used only where supported and diagnostics-only.
   141	- April 2026 month-end refresh: rerun `quant-build-production-dataset --refresh-monthly` after the first completed April month-end signal is available, then gate the new ML-ready artifact before making it the active research baseline.

{"summary":"Formal read-only review completed. The plan addresses real local problems, especially hardcoded 20260424 production paths and subprocess orchestration, but it needs tighter sequencing and sharper contracts before implementation.","markdown_response":"**Findings**

1. **High: Deprecation is sequenced too early for the current architecture.**  
   The plan says production orchestration must stop shelling out to old public CLIs and then require `--allow-manual-data-step` for those same CLIs ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:24), [data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:61)). Today, `quant-build-production-dataset` is exactly a subprocess command list calling those public entrypoints ([cli_build_production_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:71), [cli_build_production_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:178)). If guards land before internal step APIs are fully wired, the canonical command can break itself. Sequence this explicitly: first extract shared callable step APIs, then move production orchestration to them, then add direct-CLI guards.

2. **High: ML-ready boundary is under-specified and can produce incomplete labels.**  
   The plan says build/gate ML-ready when “monthly signal plus next rebalance-day data exists” ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:23)). Current labels use `next_entry_open`/`next_rebalance_date` as the label exit horizon ([labels.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/labels.py:13), [labels.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/labels.py:67)), and rows with missing exit price are excluded ([labels.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/labels.py:107)). For a newly completed month-end, having the next rebalance entry date is not necessarily enough to label that month’s return; the exit boundary may require the following month’s rebalance. Split this into `feature_snapshot_end`, `entry_data_end`, and `label_complete_through`, and decide whether the latest month is prediction-only or excluded from gated training labels.

3. **High: Freshness probing is correct in intent but too broad and ambiguous.**  
   The plan requires daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limits, ETF daily, fund_adj, fund_nav, benchmark, and all configured non-Level-2 families to be fresh before live steps run ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:58)). Current `quant-validate-tushare` only checks `stock_basic` access and all-A-share counts ([cli_validate_tushare.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py:12)). Define endpoint-specific requiredness, expected lag, retry/backoff, and whether each stale family blocks `daily-refresh`, `monthly-refresh`, or only `full-refresh`. Otherwise slower or lower-priority non-L2 endpoints can make daily execution refresh flaky.

4. **Medium: `--skip-gdelt-doc-api` conflicts with current default behavior.**  
   The plan frames GDELT DOC API as part of default macro/events with a skip flag ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:14), [data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:21)). Current macro builder defaults GDELT DOC API off and only fetches it with `--enable-gdelt-doc-api` ([cli_build_macro_event_layer.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_macro_event_layer.py:20), [cli_build_macro_event_layer.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_macro_event_layer.py:45)). Keep DOC API opt-in, or restrict default fetching to `full-refresh`; otherwise the daily canonical path adds avoidable network latency and external-service fragility.

5. **Medium: Official industry source discovery should not run by default on every daily refresh.**  
   The plan includes official industry manifest discovery/build in the unified dependency chain ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:20)). The current command discovers and downloads public PDF sources, then writes source files and manifests under `sources/` ([cli_build_official_industry_manifest.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_official_industry_manifest.py:55), [cli_build_official_industry_manifest.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_official_industry_manifest.py:64)). That is a mutable external fetch, not a cheap deterministic daily step. Default should reuse a pinned manifest unless coverage/staleness checks prove discovery is required.

6. **Medium: Manual bypass policy needs a concrete per-CLI contract.**  
   The plan says bypass runs must use “real arguments” and not stale defaults ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:81)), but many builder CLIs currently encode production-sensitive defaults, such as ML dataset inputs ([cli_build_ml_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_ml_dataset.py:35)), execution-ready inputs ([cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:76)), and fundamental feature dates ([cli_build_fundamental_features.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_fundamental_features.py:13)). Add a central helper that declares required explicit args per CLI under `--allow-manual-data-step`, then test each wrapper.

7. **Low: The “only default public refresh command” list has gaps.**  
   The deprecation list covers many refresh/build CLIs, but `quant-import-benchmark-constituents` remains an installed data-writing entrypoint ([pyproject.toml](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/pyproject.toml:49)) and can affect benchmark artifacts. If manual official imports are intentionally allowed, state that they are not refresh commands; otherwise include them in the manual-step policy.

8. **Info: The 2026-05-02 date fixture is valid.**  
   I checked the official SSE Labor Day notice. SSE states May 1 to May 5, 2026 are closed and normal opening resumes May 6, so resolving Saturday May 2, 2026 back to April 30, 2026 is consistent with the exchange notice: https://www.sse.com.cn/disclosure/announcement/general/c/c_20260423_10816345.shtml.

**Overall**

The plan is directionally sound and addresses real local issues: the current production command still has fixed `20260424` defaults and shells out to public CLIs. The highest-risk parts are sequencing, ML label completeness, and broad live freshness gates. I would implement this as staged work: context/date resolver plus dry-run schema first, internal step API migration second, then deprecation guards and checkpoint/resume after the unified command is self-contained.
